Run a development server: builds once, watches content/theme/config, rebuilds on change, and live-reloads the browser

Usage:
  nectar dev [--config <path>] [--port <n>] [--host <host>] [--json]

Options:
  -c, --config <path> Config path(s); repeat or comma-separate to deep-merge in order
  -p, --port <n>      Port to listen on (0..65535 integer; defaults to 4321; pass 0 to let the kernel pick a free port for CI/smoke tests)
  --host <host>       Hostname to bind to (defaults to localhost; pass 0.0.0.0 to expose on the LAN)
  -j, --json          Switch logger output (status / rebuild events) to one JSON object per line for CI / log forwarders. Accepted globally; flag here just makes it visible in `--help`
  -h, --help          Show help for this command

Environment variables:
  Every flag has an env var fallback (CLI flag > env var > config > default).
  Naming: NECTAR_<COMMAND>_<FLAG> (uppercased, dashes become underscores).
  Example: --config → NECTAR_DEV_CONFIG

Repeated flags:
  Scalar string flags use the last value.
  List-style string flags accumulate as comma-separated values in argv order.
  Boolean flags use the last positive or negated spelling.

Examples:
  nectar dev                                   # http://localhost:4321 with live reload
  nectar dev --port 8080                       # pick a different port
  nectar dev --host 0.0.0.0                    # expose on the LAN (mobile testing)
